Hello! 🌺 I am here on behalf of a writer and reader community with over 2300 members. I invite you to join our Discord server, "Writers' Encyclopedia" which is based on chatting, entertainment, games, books, and critiques, and is active all day long. We would be delighted to see you among the carefully selected writers and readers in our server. Our server consists of undiscovered and developing writers and quality readers. Five days a week, three chapters of a writer's book are read and discussed in turn. There are two optional tours in the server: "critique" and "support" tours. In the critique tour, we point out where the writer is lacking and where they are strong. The support tour is for our members who are preparing for exams and do not have much time. In this tour, reading is not mandatory, and the tasks do not take much time.

We strive to create the best conditions for writers with low interaction. We organize competitions in our server and give rewards like comments and votes to the winners. We also prepare events in the server. Additionally, we hold a "writer of the month" contest, and the winner receives comment and vote rewards. 

Currently, we have over 2300 members and are always active. We have writers of all ages, from 13 to 45, in our server, with the majority being between 13 and 30. We socialize almost every day with activities like role-playing and theater. In our different game activity channels, we play games like mafia, vampire village, and murder mystery with the members. For fun, we also have games like Owo, chess, word generation, and hangman. Every night, members gather in the voice channel to listen to music.


To join our server, write down to copy the link above (https:// discord .com/invite/M2PUn4GY2q), paste it on instagram or discord, and click on it. If you don't understand just ask. This link never expires, and you can join anytime. You can also click the link from my clipboard.

Hey.. so I usually don't write comments or anything, but I got this feeling that I want to say this. You know, when they said that "some  books could get you speechless" I was like, "huh". It didn't happen. Until now. I've finished this book recently and I was like, "What am I gonna do?" I literally sat on my chair and zoned into the wall. This was so unbelievably well written. Not just the language, by that I mean grammar yk. But the whole idea, the whole thing. It was raw, it was honest and just as you said, it was real. At least for a moment, but that's just how life is, right? The intensity, the contrast between protagonists, the backstory. 
If your goal was to make readers feel the story, to feel every little emotion and every little change in behavior, you succeeded. At least with me.
I thought about continuing and as you said, this is the whole thing. Maybe the continuing would ruin this, maybe not. But it's the whole thing. There's coldness, there's warmth, there's rawness, there's understanding, love and eventually - a breaking point with grief.
I know a lot of people told you that it was great, but I got the need to tell you as well.
I hope this makes sense, english is not my first language, so I'm sorry for errors.

I honestly expected them to get back together like how most of the stories I've read ended up. But honestly, I agree with this—I agree with you, Author. Not every story needs a happy ending—an ending where leads are meant to be together. Honestly, as a reader myself, I did hope that at the end, it would be Jo and Val too, but, having them to end up together dissolves the purpose of this story—to portray something real, hard to accept but definitely SOMETHING REAL. 

Yes, we did enjoy those little banters and sweet moments  they had, but they both deserve to grow and live a life where they both feel secured and at peace, apparently, they did not find that within each other. I am NOT implying that  they  need to  find it in another person, but rather  finally finding the peace and happiness they deserve within themselves.

 Let us now give Jo and Val the  closure they both deserve, my fellow readers. They both suffered enough. Let's let them rest now.

Hi @pencilpoetry

I can’t seem to get over this story, because I’ve experienced some  thing similar myself and found so much of Jo in me. I didn’t get my happy ending either, and a part of me was hoping that they would, just so I could imagine what it might feel like — even if only through your book/fiction. 

But I truly understand the direction you chose for this story. It gave me a lot to think about, and I want to sincerely thank you for that. As someone who has gone through something so close to what the character faced, I’ve learned and healed through their perspective in ways I didn’t expect. 

I also want to say — your writing has set the bar so high for me. I struggled to pick a new books now, because nothing quite compares. I always end up coming back to your profile, waiting for updates. Your work has left a lasting mark in me, and I’m deeply grateful for it. 🙏🏻
